ground_truth.title: The Most Affordable Online Master’s in ArtificiaI Intelligence Programs
ground_truth.main_content:
It’s hard to look anywhere online and not see the mention of artificial intelligence or AI. Technology is having massive impacts on everything from healthcare to how people make art.

The explosion of artificial intelligence also opens up new kinds of jobs and career options. 

One of the best ways to jumpstart or rapidly transition a career into artificial intelligence is to obtain a master’s degree. 

Here are the key points based on the 14 programs we’ve compiled:

- Lowest-Cost School: Colorado State University Global at $13,428.
- Average Cost: $44,107.
- Most Expensive School: Duke University at $98,970.

Cost-Effective Advantage of Online Programs
Only a few years ago, there were hardly any standalone master’s programs dedicated solely to preparing students for the unique challenges and opportunities in artificial intelligence. Today, more and more colleges and universities are quickly adapting to the surge in demand and offering new programs. 

Not only that, but increasingly, there are some great online options for obtaining a master’s in artificial intelligence. There are several advantages to pursuing an online master’s degree, and one of them is the overall cost. 

Check out this year’s list of most affordable online AI master’s degree options:

Most Affordable Online Master’s in AI Degree Programs for 2025
1. Colorado State University Global
Program: Online Master’s Degree in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $13,428
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $373
Credits: 36
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

2. University of the Cumberlands
Program: Executive Master's in Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Hybrid
Total tuition: $17,019
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $549
Credits: 31
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

3. Fairfax University of America
Program: Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $18,288
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $508
Credits: 36
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

4. Maryville University
Program: Online Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $27,225
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $825
Credits: 33
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

5. University of San Diego
Program: MS in Applied Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $28,950
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $965
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

6. Pennsylvania State University
Program: Master of Professional Studies in Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $35,211
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $1,067
Credits: 33
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

7. DePaul University
Program: MS in Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online & campus
Total tuition: $38,208
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $796
Credits: 48
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

8. Rochester Institute of Technology
Program: Master of Science Degree in Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online & campus
Total tuition: $42,090
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $1,403
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Required for individuals with degrees from international universities
Learn more: Program details

9. Illinois Institute of Technology
Program: Artificial Intelligence (M.A.S.)
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online & campus
Total tuition: $53,400
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $1,780
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

10. University of Texas at Austin
Program: Master’s of Artificial Intelligence
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $57,390
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $1,248 in-state | $1,913 out-of-state
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Required
Learn more: Program details

11. Stevens Institute of Technology
Program: Applied Artificial Intelligence Master's Program
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online & campus
Total tuition: $57,900
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $1,930
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Required
Learn more: Program details

12. Drexel University
Program: Master's in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online & campus
Total tuition: $64,710
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $1,438
Credits: 45
GRE requirement: Recommended for international students and for domestic students with a GPA below 3.0
Learn more: Program details

13. Johns Hopkins University
Program: Artificial Intelligence Master's Program Online
ARTiBA accreditation: No
Delivery method: Online
Total tuition: $64,710
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $2,157
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

14. Duke University
Program: MEng AI
ARTiBA accreditation: Yes
Delivery method: Online & campus
Total tuition: $98,970
2024/2025 Cost per credit: $3,299
Credits: 30
GRE requirement: Not required
Learn more: Program details

Artificial Intelligence Scholarships
AFCEA STEM Majors Scholarships for Undergraduate Students
The AFCEA STEM Majors Scholarships provide financial support to undergraduate students pursuing degrees in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). These scholarships help students develop careers in national security, defense, and technology innovation.
Amount: $2,500
Deadline: May 2025
Awards Available: 1
More information

Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI) Student Scholarships
The AAAI Student Scholarships assist students in attending AAAI conferences, presenting research, and engaging with AI professionals. These scholarships help students expand their knowledge and network in artificial intelligence.
Amount: Varies
Deadline: December 2025
Awards Available: Varies 
More information

Google PhD Fellowship Program
The Google PhD Fellowship Program recognizes and funds exceptional PhD students in computer science, AI, and related fields. Fellows receive financial support and mentorship from Google researchers.
Amount: $42,000
Deadline: September 2024
Awards Available: Varies 
More information

Great Minds in STEM Scholarship
The Great Minds in STEM (GMiS) Scholarship provides financial assistance to underrepresented students pursuing STEM degrees. It supports academic excellence, leadership, and contributions to the STEM community.
Amount: $500–$5,000
Deadline: April 2025
Awards Available: Multiple 
More information

IBM PhD Fellowship
The IBM PhD Fellowship provides financial support, mentorship, and research collaboration for outstanding PhD students in AI, quantum computing, cybersecurity, and cloud computing. It connects students with IBM researchers to drive innovation.
Amount: $60,000
Deadline: August 2024
Awards Available: Varies
More information

Joanne Pransky Celebration of Women in Robotics
The Joanne Pransky Celebration of Women in Robotics honors and supports women pursuing careers in robotics by offering mentorship, financial assistance, and professional development opportunities. It promotes diversity and leadership in robotics.
Amount: $1,000
Deadline: February 2025
Awards Available: 10 
More information

Microsoft Research PhD Fellowship
The Microsoft Research PhD Fellowship supports outstanding PhD students conducting research in computing and related fields. Recipients receive funding, mentorship from Microsoft researchers, and career development opportunities.
Amount: $42,000
Deadline: July 2025
Awards Available: Varies 
More information

NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship Program
The NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship Program provides funding and research support to PhD students working on AI, deep learning, and GPU computing. Recipients gain access to NVIDIA’s cutting-edge technologies and mentorship opportunities.
Amount: up to $60,000 
Deadline: September 2025
Awards Available: Varies 
More information

SWE Collegiate/Graduate Scholars
The SWE Collegiate/Graduate Scholars program awards scholarships to women pursuing undergraduate and graduate degrees in engineering, computing, and technology. It encourages gender diversity and career success in STEM.
Amount: $1,000 to $3,000
Deadline: October 2025
Awards Available: 2
More information

UPE/ACM Scholarship Award
The UPE/ACM Scholarship Award is a joint initiative by Upsilon Pi Epsilon and the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) to support exceptional students in computing. This scholarship recognizes academic merit and leadership in computer science.
Amount: $1,500
Deadline: September 2025
Awards Available: 4
More information
